source_paper: LegalHalluLens: Typed Hallucination Auditing and Calibrated Multi-Agent Debate for Trustworthy Legal AI

claims.claim_text: LLMs exhibit ~52% aggregate hallucination rate on legal contract extraction
Aggregate metrics conceal typed failure ordering and are insufficient for deployment decisions

evidence.section: 13. Results: Aggregate Rates Cannot Support Deployment Decisions

claims.claim_text: Failure ordering {numeric, obligation} ≫ factual ≥ temporal holds consistently across 4 architecturally diverse models

evidence.section: 14. Results: The Typed Failure Ordering Is Consistent and Large

claims.claim_text: Within-model typed gap spans 38–41 percentage points across all tested models

evidence.section: 14. Results: The Typed Failure Ordering Is Consistent and Large

claims.claim_text: Two models with matched ~52% aggregate Hal_TP can carry opposite Risk Direction Index values
RDI for gpt-5.2 is +0.161 (invention-heavy); RDI for qwen3-32b is −0.202 (omission-heavy); 95% CIs do not overlap
Scope errors dominate 62–71% of all content contradictions across all models
The typed debate pipeline reduces fabricated detections by 45% (524 → 287) on the matched subset
The debate pipeline moves gemma-4-26B-A4B from last place (Score 5.2) to first (Score 2.4) in composite score
Per-type ΔFAR gains match the ordering predicted by Experiment 1: obligation > factual > numeric > temporal
Obligation RDI shifts from −0.078 (omission-heavy) to −0.014 (near-balanced) after applying typed debate
The benchmark evaluates 249,252 clause-level instances across 510 contracts × 3 runs × 4 models
gemma-4-26B-A4B (4B active parameters) matches or exceeds commercial APIs, ranking #1 under 4 of 5 weighting schemes
Content contradictions barely changed (642 → 641, −0.2%) — the pipeline filters fabrications, not content errors
llama-3.3-70b achieves only 12.1% numeric JEq — fewer than 1 in 8 numeric clauses correctly extracted with correct content

evidence.section: 14. Results: The Typed Failure Ordering Is Consistent and Large

claims.claim_text: The debate pipeline uses a state machine with six agent roles: Skeptic, Supporter, Re-extractor, Arbiter, Verifier, Judge
Typed Skeptic challenges are derived from per-category failure modes identified in Experiment 1, not from general-purpose heuristics
CUAD v1.0 provides 510 commercial contracts with 41 expert-annotated clause types as a complete oracle
Missing rows (0.2–1.9%) are contract-correlated rather than random — 5 contracts fail across all 3 qwen3-32b runs
Per-run standard deviations are small relative to the typed gap (max 0.6 pp on Hal_TP), confirming stability

evidence.section: 14. Results: The Typed Failure Ordering Is Consistent and Large
